game_name: Disney's Magical Quest 3 Starring Mickey & Donald

danger_signals.visual_form: Character sprites of hostile creatures throughout levels
danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat requiring combat or avoidance

danger_signals.visual_form: Enlarged antagonist sprite, distinct from standard enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Critical danger point; defeating Pete is required to progress and rescue Pluto

danger_signals.visual_form: Cliff edges, pit bottomless sections in level design

danger_signals.meaning: Damage source; requires firefighter outfit with water spray to traverse safely

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible outfit items placed at level start/progression points
safety_signals.meaning: Empowerment state; each outfit grants unique abilities for surviving upcoming challenges
Safe zones where player can spend coins to upgrade outfits and purchase items

safety_signals.visual_form: Special heart items scattered in levels
safety_signals.meaning: Expansion of player health meter; increases survivability
Resource accumulation for purchasing safety upgrades and outfit improvements
Magical meter replenishment; sustains offensive capability
Water meter replenishment; sustains environmental hazard mitigation

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mickey Mouse character identity; Donald Duck in MQ3 variant
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Starting position; rescued Pluto at journey's end

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Pete character sprite; ruler of magical land
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss-form Pete with enlarged/distinct sprite presentation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Pete's domain; the magical land where Pluto is held captive

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Goofy sprite (provides mountain-climbing gear); Old wizard sprite (provides quest context)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appear at specific progression points; Goofy in level 4

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Standard platforming environment with scattered enemies, coins, and obstacles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player freely moves through levels; enemies patrol or attack on contact

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Flame effects visible in environment sections; visual barrier of fire
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must equip firefighter outfit to spray water and extinguish flames; alternative paths blocked

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Magical enemies or magical environmental puzzles requiring charged magic blasts
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must equip magician outfit; charging system for power escalation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tall structures, cliff faces, or elevated platforms
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must equip mountain-climbing gear with grappling hook to progress upward
Player can breathe and move freely underwater only with magician outfit equipped

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition to enlarged boss arena; Pete sprite dominates visual space
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat-locked state; Pete presents complex attack patterns requiring equipped outfit abilities

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dream sequence ends; return to normal bedroom setting
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: State transition to happy ending; Mickey wakes, friends present

environmental_storytelling.example: Game begins with innocent catch game in park; Mickey falls into magical land; ends with dream revelation. The entire adventure is contextualized as a dream, creating temporal and reality narrative markers.

Magician outfit awarded in level 2, firefighter uniform found in level 3, mountain-climbing gear given by Goofy in level 4. Outfit availability signals which level introduces which environmental challenge type.
Old wizard appears to explain Pete's threat and Pluto's capture. Goofy appears in level 4 to provide climbing gear. NPCs visually signal ally relationships and provide mechanical/narrative context.
Fire hazards visually present in level 3, immediately followed by firefighter outfit acquisition. Visual environmental threat directly prompts mechanical solution visibility.
Coins, hearts, and magical lamps distributed throughout levels communicate game progression philosophy: persistent resource collection enables persistent advancement and survivability.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can grab and spin-throw blocks at enemies; basic platforming mobility
Fires charged magic blasts; larger blasts with longer charge; drains magic meter; can breathe underwater; can control magic carpets
Sprays water streams at enemies; extinguishes fires; pushes blocks; forms icicles; drains water meter on use
Uses grappling hook to attach to objects; hoists/swings upward; grabs items and enemies; serves as weapon for spin-throw
Patrol level areas; attack on player contact; defeatable by jumping or block-throw
Complex attack patterns; requires outfit-specific abilities to defeat; represents culminating challenge
Remains static in Pete's domain; visual goal marker; rescue triggers ending sequence

notes: Disney's Magical Quest 3 uses a costume/outfit system as primary mechanic for world-state communication. Each outfit not only grants distinct abilities but visually signals to player which environmental challenges are ahead. The game communicates danger through enemy presence and environmental hazards (fire, water, height), while safety is signaled through resource collection (coins, hearts, magical items) and shop locations. Pete serves as the central antagonist faction marker. The dream-frame narrative creates a meta-level world-state communication: the entire adventure's reality is contextual. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on outfit placement timing and NPC appearance sequencing rather than detailed environmental dressing. The simple SNES graphical style limits visual complexity, so communication relies on sprite distinctiveness, color coding of items, and level design spatial clarity.
